rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports belong in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annexes only in valid scopes matching component type or implementation.
Use only visible identifiers from the current AADL component scope in AGREE annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or Deadline as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should appear after relevant subcomponents and connections in the implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level AGREE bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE contract validation and repair patterns
AADL annex placement rules for assume/guarantee syntax
